data.title: Here's What the Big Bank CEOs Got Paid in 2025 — WSJ
data.description:
By Gina Heeb and AnnaMaria Andriotis

The chief executives of the largest U.S. banks together raked in $258 million in compensation for 2025, after a strong economy and buoyant Wall Street propelled their businesses to record levels.

The CEOs of JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley and Wells Fargo each earned $40 million or more last year, a collective increase of more than 21% from 2024.

Morgan Stanley's Ted Pick and Wells Fargo's Charlie Scharf saw the biggest pay bumps at 32% and 28%, respectively.

In 2025, the six banks brought in a total of nearly $600 billion in revenue, a 6% increase from a year earlier and more than any year prior. Profits across the banks rose 8%.

At JPMorgan, compensation for Jamie Dimon rose 10% to $43 million. The 69-year-old has overseen the expansion of the bank into the nation's largest over his two decades as CEO. He hasn't said when he plans to step down.

Much of the earnings lift industrywide came from a surge in dealmaking and trading volume, with the latter benefiting from market volatility sparked by President Trump's tariffs and other geopolitical uncertainty. Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley posted record annual revenues in their divisions that house these operations.

At Wells Fargo, Scharf has also moved to increase its Wall Street businesses since the Federal Reserve released the bank from a restriction that had capped its assets.

After a tumultuous start to 2025, corporate chieftains regained the confidence to pursue deals, resulting in the second-highest mergers volume on record in 2025. That fueled a rise in lending that helps make these deals happen. Companies are also borrowing at a record clip to pay for artificial intelligence and other infrastructure build-outs, turning to big banks and other sectors of Wall Street.

Households have also continued to spend and borrow, generating interest and fee charges for the big banks that cater to Main Street. In December, average hourly earnings in the U.S. were up 3.8% from a year earlier.

Big bank executives have made bullish forecasts for 2026.

Under Trump, banks have benefited from a looser regulatory stance toward deals, capital and oversight. Bankers are also hoping that 2026 could be the biggest year ever for IPOs, with rocket maker SpaceX, artificial-intelligence company Anthropic and others eyeing listings.

Still, bankers are warning of the possibility of challenges ahead.

Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon said Tuesday he would expect a "speed bump or recalibration or slowdown" at some point this year, and flagged the U.S. midterm elections and "populist uncertainty" potentially introducing new risks.

Write to Gina Heeb at gina.heeb@wsj.com and AnnaMaria Andriotis at annamaria.andriotis@wsj.com

data.title: Enbridge CEO Signals Little Interest to Build New Canada Pipeline
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

The chief executive of pipeline operator Enbridge says he's not keen to pursue energy-infrastructure projects with considerable political risk.

Those remarks likely rule Enbridge out as a possible private-sector builder of a new pipeline the province of Alberta is championing, which as envisaged would carry crude oil from the province to the northern British Columbia coast. The province of British Columbia has voiced its opposition, while the federal Canadian government said it could back a new crude-oil corridor under certain circumstances.

"I don't think investors or the infrastructure companies should be taking on all that risk of the development in jurisdictions that have historically created" political and policy challenges, said Gregory Ebel, Enbridge CEO, in a conference call with analysts Friday to discuss fourth-quarter earnings.

He cited Enbridge's previous experience in trying to develop the Northern Gateway pipeline, which had the backing of a former federal Conservative government. After spending over $400 million, Ebel said the company had to abandon the project in 2016 after former Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said he would not approve Northern Gateway. The project, which faced considerable opposition from indigenous communities and environmentalists, also faced setbacks in Canadian courts.

The new pipeline Alberta is looking to build is "not the type of risk that we're looking to take on at this time. You don't need to with other all the other opportunities," Ebel said.

Alberta and the federal Canadian government signed an agreement last year, in which Prime Minister Mark Carney offered conditional support for the pipeline that Alberta Premier Danielle Smith wants constructed. Canada agreed to suspend some environmental rules for Alberta, most notably a cap on carbon emissions for the oil-and-gas sector, and Alberta agreed to adopt a more stringent industrial carbon-pricing system. Carney added Ottawa's approval was conditional on finding a private-sector proponent to lead the pipeline, and a partnership with indigenous communities to ensure they had equity ownership.

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

data.title: FONAR CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
FONAR Corp, a leading manufacturer and servicer of MRI equipment and provider of diagnostic facilities management services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, providing insights into its revenue growth, operational challenges, and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $51.6 million, increased by 3.3% from $50.0 million for the six months ended December 31, 2024, driven by increases in management and other fees.

Operating Income: $6.2 million, decreased from $7.0 million for the six months ended December 31, 2024, due to costs and expenses increasing at a larger rate than revenues.

Net Income: $5.2 million, decreased from $6.2 million for the six months ended December 31, 2024, reflecting the impact of increased costs and expenses.

Net Income for the three months ended December 31, 2025: $2.5 million, increased from $2.2 million for the three months ended December 31, 2024, due to a small increase in revenues.

Operating Income for the three months ended December 31, 2025: $3.0 million, increased from $2.4 million for the three months ended December 31, 2024, as revenues increased while total costs and expenses remained constant.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company operates in two reportable segments: the manufacture and servicing of MRI equipment, and diagnostic facilities management services. Revenue from product sales and service and repair fees increased from $4.0 million to $4.9 million, while management and other fees increased from $30.5 million to $31.8 million.

Geographical Performance: The company manages or owns a total of 45 MRI scanners, with 27 located in New York and 18 in Florida. The absence of hurricane-related closures contributed to an increase in the number of scans performed.

Sales Units: The aggregate number of scans performed by the sites owned and managed increased to 109,952 scans from 106,168 scans, indicating operational growth in the diagnostic facilities management segment.

Operational Challenges: The company faces challenges due to stagnant Medicare reimbursement rates for MRI scans, which are flat year over year for 2026, while operational costs, particularly for MRI technologist and radiologist compensation, have been increasing.

Future Outlook: The company plans to open an additional location on Long Island, New York, with expected costs of approximately $400,000 for a new scanner and $500,000 in related buildout costs. This expansion is part of the company's commitment to improving and expanding MRI facilities.

Operational Improvements: The company has made expenditures related to information technology software and cybersecurity improvements in response to deficiencies identified during the most recent audit.

Service and Repair: Service and repair fees revenue increased to $4.5 million from $3.8 million, with costs relating to providing service increasing to $2.3 million. The company attributes this to spending on a subsidiary dedicated to the maintenance and repair of non-FONAR MRI equipment.

Market Conditions: Economic uncertainty and lower reimbursement rates for MRI scans have depressed the market for MRI scanner products, despite the unique technological capabilities of the company's scanners.

Commitment to Quality: The company is committed to providing enhanced equipment service and maintenance capabilities and delivering state-of-the-art, innovative, and high-quality equipment and upgrades at competitive prices.

SEC Filing: FONAR CORP [ FONR ] - 10-Q - Feb. 13, 2026

data.title: Sphere Arena's Success Is Sweet for Combative CEO James Dolan — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Andrew Bary

The Sphere arena's success in Las Vegas has been a boon to investors and vindication for the company's CEO James Dolan, who conceived it, helped design it, and followed its construction with an on-site camera linked to his Manhattan office.

Shares of Sphere Entertainment, the owner of the high-tech entertainment venue, jumped 22% Thursday after the company reported better-than- expected results for the fourth-quarter, powered by strong ticket sales of The Wizard of Oz, an immersive version of the classic movie shown at the Sphere.

The stock was little changed Friday at $115.70 after hitting a record high earlier in the session, and now has tripled over the past year.

Wolfe Research analyst Peter Supino, one of the biggest Wall Street bulls on the stock, wrote that Oz results "blew through expectations" and he lifted his profit estimates for 2026 while expressing optimism on Sphere's new arenas planned for Abu Dhabi and the Washington, D.C., area. He upped his price target to $147 a share. He sees about $300 million of adjusted operating income for the company this year.

In addition to running Sphere, Dolan is CEO of both Madison Square Garden Sports, the owner of the New York Knicks and Rangers, and Madison Square Garden Entertainment, the owner of the famed Manhattan arena.

He's not been overly popular in the investment community. The sometimes combative 70-year-old is the leader of the Dolan family, which has maintained ironclad control over its companies through super-voting shares. His at-times brusque behavior has been covered in the New York media.

Investors worry that he has put control over shareholder value creation at MSG Sports, which continues to trade at about half its estimated asset value even after a 50% gain in its shares in the past six months. Wall Street refers to a "Dolan discount" to the family-controlled companies.

The Sphere offered James Dolan an opportunity to create something new and separate himself from the legacy of his late father, Charles Dolan, who built Cablevision into the leading Long Island cable company before selling it. Charles Dolan bought the Knicks and Rangers about 30 years ago from Sumner Redstone's Viacom at a fraction of their current value.

The Sphere got off to a somewhat rocky start in September 2023 after construction costs ballooned to $2.3 billion, about double the initial estimates.

While the arena drew raves about its sound system, there were doubts that it would earn a decent return on investment from concerts and original programming like Postcard From Earth, an immersive nature show. Those concerns persisted into 2025.

Barron's wrote favorably on the company in May 2025, arguing that investors were "paying little for its potential" with a market value then of $1.2 billion, half the construction cost. The company now is valued at over $5 billion assuming issuance of shares from a $259 million convertible bond.

"For around $1 billion, investors can own what could be a global network of differentiated properties," Supino said at the time.

What has changed the story is the success of Oz. Ariel Investment co-CEO John Rogers, whose firm is a big Sphere holder, correctly predicted that Oz would be a hit, saying last May viewers will "feel like they're in the cyclone with Dorothy." Oz has sold 2.2 million tickets for $290 million after opening around Labor Day.

The company is in excellent shape financially with net cash on its balance sheet excluding the convertible, and it plans to contribute a much smaller percentage of the costs of building the Virginia and Abu Dhabi Spheres than at the Vegas Sphere.

Dolan said on the earnings call earlier this week that he sees the potential for "five or six projects going on at once." Dolan declined to comment to Barron's.

Supino sees each new Sphere worth $10 to $20 a share for the company from royalty and other income, as well as the ability to leverage content creation costs over more venues.

The stock is no longer cheap after its run-up, but there could be more upside as additional Spheres are built.

"Overall, today's results further validate the SPHR model and long-term potential, and amid a market rattled by AI, highlight the relative safety of live entertainment," wrote JP Morgan analyst David Karnovsky earlier this week.

Write to Andrew Bary at andrew.bary@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Envoy Medical Signs Multiple Financing Agreements
data.description:
Envoy Medical announced material agreements supporting a best efforts public offering of common stock and warrants. Under a Securities Purchase Agreement dated Feb 11, 2026, the company raised approximately $30.0 million in gross proceeds at $0.40 per share (or $0.3999 per pre-funded warrant), with additional proceeds possible upon warrant exercises. Insiders participated alongside other investors. Envoy also maintained its engagement of H.C. Wainwright & Co. as exclusive placement agent, agreeing to a 7% fee and issuing placement agent warrants. Proceeds will support working capital and Envoy’s FDA pivotal study for its Acclaim CI device.

Agreement 1: Envoy Medical Signs Securities Purchase Agreement With Multiple Investors

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement for public offering of common stock and warrants
  • Counterparty: Multiple Investors
  • Signed / Effective: Feb 11 2026 / Feb 12 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Raise capital for working capital and clinical study

Agreement 2: Envoy Medical Signs Placement Agent Engagement Letter With H.C. Wainwright & Co.

  • Agreement type: Placement agent engagement letter (as amended)
  • Counterparty: H.C. Wainwright & Co.
  • Signed / Effective: Sep 17 2025 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until completion of offering
  • Reason: Facilitate execution and distribution of capital raise

Original SEC Filing: Envoy Medical, Inc. [ COCH ] - 8-K - Feb. 13, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Charlie's Holdings Signs Subscription Agreements for Equity Financing With Investors
data.description:
Charlie's Holdings entered into subscription agreements for a private placement of 3,550,000 shares of common stock at $0.20 per share. The Company received $510,000 in cash proceeds and $200,000 through debt forgiveness. The financing, conducted under Section 4(a)(2) of the Securities Act, will be used for working capital purposes.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Private placement subscription agreements for common stock
  • Counterparty: Investors
  • Signed / Effective: Feb 13 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Raise working capital and strengthen liquidity

Original SEC Filing: Charlie's Holdings, Inc. [ CHUC ] - 8-K - Feb. 13, 2026

data.title: Cathay General Bancorp Raises Dividend by 12%
data.description:
By Amira McKee

Cathay General Bancorp raised its quarterly dividend by 12% to 38 cents a share from 34 cents.

The new payout, equal to $1.52 a year, represents an annual yield of about 2.9% based on the latest closing price of $53.33.

The dividend is payable on March 9 to stockholders of record at the close of business on Feb. 26, the company said Friday.

Write to Amira McKee at amira.mckee@wsj.com

data.title: ATN International Signs Multiple Material Agreements
data.description:
ATN International announced two material agreements supporting the planned sale of approximately 214 wireless tower sites. The company signed a Purchase and Sale Agreement with Everest Infrastructure Partners for up to $297 million in cash, with an initial closing expected in the second quarter of 2026 and subsequent closings for deferred sites. ATN will lease back tower space to maintain service and will serve as Everest’s preferred backhaul provider at the assigned sites. Separately, ATN entered into a Consent Agreement with CoBank and other required lenders to approve the transaction, permit distributions and use of proceeds, prioritize repayment of the Revolving A-1 Loan, and release liens on the assets being sold.

Agreement 1: ATN International Signs Purchase and Sale Agreement With Everest Infrastructure Partners

  • Agreement type: Purchase and Sale Agreement for ~214 wireless tower sites with leaseback and backhaul preference
  • Counterparty: Everest Infrastructure Partners
  • Signed / Effective: Feb 11 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Monetize tower portfolio and retain network access

Agreement 2: ATN International Signs Consent Agreement With CoBank

  • Agreement type: Consent under existing Credit Agreement for asset sale, proceed uses, and lien releases
  • Counterparty: CoBank and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Feb 11 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: N/A
  • Reason: Facilitate asset sale and optimize proceeds usage

Original SEC Filing: ATN International, Inc. [ ATNI ] - 8-K - Feb. 13, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: S&P 500 ends up slightly as tech dips, inflation cools
data.description:
Key points:
  • Indexes: Dow up 0.10%, S&P 500 up 0.05%; Nasdaq off 0.22%
  • US inflation increases less than expected in January
  • Applied Materials jumps after upbeat Q2 earnings forecast
  • Defensive utilities, real estate sectors outperform

By Sinéad Carew and Twesha Dikshit

The S&P 500 closed barely higher on Friday, supported by cooling inflation data, but the Nasdaq ended lower as heavyweight technology and communications services shares lost ground on nagging fears of disruption by artificial intelligence.

The S&P 500, the Nasdaq and the Dow all declined for the week with technology stocks on a roller-coaster ride due to uncertainty about the extent to which profits could be disrupted due to AI competition and the hefty spending needed to support the technology.

Equities had started the session strong after data showed U.S. consumer prices increased less than expected in January. This prompted traders to slightly raise the chance of a 25 basis point interest-rate cut in June to 52.3% from 48.9%, according to the CME Group's FedWatch tool.

But heavyweight technology and communications services ended the session lower as investors were jittery ahead of Monday's U.S. holiday for Presidents Day.

"Large cap tech stocks continue to be an anchor on the market and any whiff of optimism continues to get rejected," said Michael James, managing director, at Rosenblatt Securities, Los Angeles.

"We've been on wobbly legs a couple of weeks now and with the three-day weekend approaching, it's not surprising to roll over into the end of the day."

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 48.95 points, or 0.10%, to 49,500.93, the S&P 500 gained 3.41 points, or 0.05%, to 6,836.17 and the Nasdaq Composite lost 50.48 points, or 0.22%, to 22,546.67.

For the week, the S&P 500 fell 1.39%, the Nasdaq declined 2.1%, and the Dow fell 1.23% for their biggest weekly losses since November.

Equity markets have pulled back from record levels recently as AI fears fueled worries in sectors spanning from software and insurance to trucking companies. However, the S&P 500 software and services index (.SPLRCIS) closed up 0.9% on Friday while the S&P 500 tech sector fell 0.5%.

Despite improving inflation trends, Phil Orlando, chief market strategist at Federated Hermes, predicted more choppy trading ahead as investors deal with the looming U.S. midterm elections in November and the expected replacement of Fed Chair Jerome Powell by Kevin Warsh in May.

Historically when a Fed leadership transition happens in a midterm year, the market has hit a "double-digit air-pocket every time that's occurred," Orlando said.

Megacap tech stocks were weak with Nvidia and Apple Inc providing the biggest drags to the S&P 500 while Applied Materials provided the strongest boost.

Defensive utilities ended up 2.69% and real estate added 1.48%, making them the top gainers among S&P 500's 11 major industry indexes.

Healthcare was also a boost with Dexcom rising 7.6% and Moderna rising 5.3% after both companies' fourth-quarter earnings reports impressed.

Applied Materials shares jumped 8.1% after the chipmaking-equipment firm forecast second-quarter revenue and profit above Wall Street expectations.

Networking equipment provider Arista Networks gained 4.8% during the session after forecasting annual revenue above expectations.

White House trade adviser Peter Navarro said there was no basis to reports that the administration was planning to reduce steel and aluminum tariffs.

Still, some steelmakers came under pressure with Nucor falling just under 3% and Steel Dynamics slipping 3.9%. Also aluminum producer Alcoa fell 0.9% while Century Aluminum shares tumbled 7.4%.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 2.57-to-1 ratio on the NYSE where there were 392 new highs and 93 new lows. On the Nasdaq, 3,156 stocks rose and 1,646 fell as advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 1.92-to-1 ratio.

The S&P 500 posted 34 new 52-week highs and 6 new lows.

On U.S. exchanges 18.61 billion shares changed hands compared with the 20.75 billion moving average for the last 20 sessions.

data.title: Ambow Education Holding Ltd. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Ambow Education Holding Ltd., a Cayman Islands holding company with operations primarily in the U.S., has released its 2025 10-K report. The company, known for its innovative, AI-powered phygital solutions, has shown significant financial and operational growth, driven by the success of its HybriU platform. This report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business highlights, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces.

Financial Highlights

Total Net Revenues: $9.5 million, marking a 1.1% increase from 2024, primarily driven by revenue growth from the HybriU platform.

Gross Profit: $5.2 million, with a gross margin increase from 53.1% in 2024 to 54.8% in 2025, mainly due to increased revenues from HybriU.

Operating Income: $0.1 million, a significant improvement from a loss of $0.7 million in 2024, reflecting decreased operating expenses.

Net Income: $1.4 million, a substantial increase from $0.3 million in 2024, aided by a $1.5 million gain on lease settlement.

Net Income Attributable to Ordinary Shareholders: $1.4 million, reflecting the overall improvement in financial performance.

Business Highlights

Company Overview: Ambow Education Holding Ltd. focuses on shaping the future of learning, collaboration, and communication through innovative, AI-powered phygital solutions.

HybriU Platform: The HybriU platform is a cutting-edge solution that transforms education, corporate conferencing, and live events by delivering immersive, intelligent, and real-time experiences. It bridges the gap between in-person and remote interactions, enabling AI-driven automation and deep engagement.

New Product Launches: In 2023, Ambow launched HybriU, the education market’s first fully integrated, interactive AI-powered platform that bridges online and offline instruction. In 2025, the company expanded its product offerings under the HybriU brand, including HybriU Conferencing, HybriU Events, HybriU Knowledge Capture, HybriU 3D Mobile Station, WeSpeak™, HybriU Adaptive Teach, and the HybriU Global Learning Network.

Geographical Performance: The company exited its operations in China in 2022 and now focuses its operations in the United States. The launch of HybriU and its expansion into international markets through partnerships, such as the licensing agreement with Inspiring Futures Pte. LTD in Singapore, highlights its global reach.

Future Outlook: Ambow aims to lead the global shift toward smarter, more inclusive, and human-centered hybrid experiences across industries. The company plans to expand the deployment of the HybriU and WeSpeak platforms across institutional, enterprise, and event-based customers, and develop international educational initiatives through partnerships.

Research and Development: The company increased its research and development expenses from $0.4 million in 2024 to $0.6 million in 2025, primarily related to the development of the HybriU platform.

Operational Highlights: The company’s operational focus is on leveraging AI technology to drive educational advancements and revolutionize the delivery and experience of educational content. The HybriU platform is central to this mission, offering a range of solutions that integrate digital and physical environments.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: The company is focused on shaping the future of learning, collaboration, and communication through its innovative, AI-powered phygital solutions, particularly the HybriU platform. This platform is designed to bridge the gap between in-person and remote interaction, enabling AI-driven automation and seamless collaboration. The company plans to continue adding new offerings to attract students with different needs and provide cross-selling opportunities.

Capital Management: The company has been managing its capital through a combination of operating activities, bank borrowings, third-party loans, and ordinary share issuances. In 2025, the company had net cash provided by operating activities of $0.1 million and had $0.8 million in unrestricted cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2025. The company also engaged in financing activities, including $1.7 million in proceeds from short-term borrowing, offset by $1.2 million in repayments. Additionally, the company completed a change in the ADS to Class A ordinary share ratio, effectively a 1-for-10 ADS reverse split.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates needing additional capital to execute its longer-term business plan and may initiate additional public offerings, obtain credit facilities, or dispose of non-core assets to conserve liquidity. The company aims to meet its cash needs for at least the next 12 months but acknowledges the uncertainty of achieving a net income position in the foreseeable future. The company is also exploring opportunities for capital investment with an emphasis on sustainable growth initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: The company faces significant risks related to its ability to attract and retain students, which is critical for maintaining profitability. This challenge is compounded by macroeconomic factors such as unemployment and regulatory uncertainties that may affect enrollment. Additionally, the company must attract and retain qualified education professionals to maintain teaching quality, which is essential for its brand and operational success.

The company's expansion into new businesses, particularly in AI technology, presents operational and compliance challenges. The rapidly evolving nature of AI and the need for different operational systems could disrupt current operations and financial results. There are also potential reputational risks associated with AI adoption, such as flawed algorithms or biased datasets.

The company is highly dependent on its brand strength, and any negative publicity could materially affect its business. It faces intense competition in the education sector, which could lead to reduced market share and profitability. The integration of acquired businesses poses risks, including potential disruptions and failure to realize anticipated benefits.

Operational risks include potential network interruptions, security breaches, and system failures, which could damage the company's reputation and financial performance. The company is also subject to stringent privacy and data protection laws, which, if not complied with, could result in significant fines and liabilities.

Regulatory risks are significant, particularly concerning compliance with U.S. higher education regulations. Failure to comply with Title IV requirements could result in loss of access to federal student loans and grants, severely impacting the company's financial condition. The company must also navigate the complexities of the HFCA Act, which could lead to delisting from U.S. stock exchanges if audit inspections are not adequately conducted.

Management acknowledges the challenges posed by regulatory changes and competitive pressures. The company is investing in AI technology to drive future growth but recognizes the associated risks and uncertainties. Management is focused on maintaining brand strength and competitive positioning through strategic marketing and quality assurance.

The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, which could adversely affect financial results. Additionally, the company faces risks related to changes in accounting standards and taxation rules, which could impact reported results and business operations.

SEC Filing: Ambow Education Holding Ltd. [ AMBO ] - 10-K - Feb. 13, 2026

data.title: Soybean basis bids steady to weaker as futures retreat
data.description:
Spot basis bids for soybeans were flat to lower in the U.S. Midwest on Friday as futures fell back from Thursday's rally.

  • The soy basis fell at a river terminal in Morris, Illinois, and processing plants in Des Moines, Iowa and Sioux City, Iowa.

  • The corn basis rose at a river terminal in Toledo, Ohio and fell at the river terminal in Morris. It also fell at an elevator in Council Bluffs, Iowa.

  • Soybean futures retreated from multi-month highs set the previous day as traders booked profits and squared positions ahead of a long U.S. holiday weekend and the upcoming Lunar New Year holidays in China, analysts said.

  • Corn futures made some modest gains, supported by a strong U.S. export pace and concerns about grain quality in China.

  • CBOT March soybeans (SH26) settled 4-1/4 cents lower to $11.33 per bushel. CBOT March corn (CH26) settled 1/2 cent higher at $4.31-3/4 per bushel.

data.title: Apple Rumored To Equip IPhone 18 Pro Models With Custom C2 Modem
data.description:
CUPERTINO (dpa-AFX) - Apple Inc. (AAPL) is rumored to be equipping its upcoming iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max models with a next-generation in-house C2 modem, further reducing the company's reliance on Qualcomm's 5G chips used in previous iPhone models.

This shift is expected to bring significant improvements in battery life, privacy, and network performance, aligning with Apple's broader strategy of tightly integrating hardware and software.

One of the biggest anticipated benefits of the C2 chip is improved battery efficiency. Apple's earlier custom modems have emphasized power savings through deep integration with iOS and Apple silicon, allowing the processor and modem to work together more intelligently when handling cellular tasks.

While the company has not shared specific performance metrics in past launches, this integration has been credited with extending battery life. With the iPhone 18 Pro also expected to feature a larger battery, the efficiency gains from the C2 modem could further enhance overall endurance.

The new modem is also expected to support Apple's 'Limit Precise Location' feature, which reduces how accurately cellular carriers can determine a user's location. When enabled, carriers may only be able to identify a general area, such as a neighborhood, instead of a precise address, without affecting signal quality.

In addition, tighter coordination between the processor and modem could improve responsiveness in congested network conditions by prioritizing time-sensitive data, helping the device perform more smoothly in crowded or low-signal environments.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Extra Space Storage Inc. Announces 1st Quarter 2026 Dividend
data.description:
SALT LAKE CITY, Feb. 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — Extra Space Storage Inc. (the "Company") announced today that the Company's board of directors has declared a first quarter 2026 dividend of $1.62 per share on the common stock of the Company. The dividend is payable on March 31, 2026, to stockholders of record at the close of business on March 16, 2026.

About Extra Space Storage Inc.

Extra Space Storage Inc., headquartered in Salt Lake City, is a fully integrated, self-administered and self-managed real estate investment trust, and a member of the S&P 500. As of September 30, 2025, the Company owned and/or operated 4,238 self-storage properties, which comprise approximately 2.9 million units and approximately 326.9 million square feet of rentable storage space operating under the Extra Space brand. The Company offers customers a wide selection of conveniently located and secure storage units across the country, including boat storage, RV storage and business storage. It is the largest operator of self-storage properties in the United States.

For more information, please visit www.extraspace.com.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/extra-space-storage-inc-announces-1st-quarter-2026-dividend-302688099.html

SOURCE Extra Space Storage Inc.

data.title: Google Fends Off Attacks Attempting To Clone Its AI Chatbot
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Google has revealed that its flagship artificial intelligence chatbot, Google Gemini, has been the target of repeated attempts by 'commercially motivated' actors to clone the system.

These attacks involve bombarding the chatbot with large volumes of carefully crafted prompts in an effort to uncover the patterns and logic underlying its responses.

According to a report published by Google, the company has faced a rise in so-called 'distillation attacks,' also known as model extraction. The goal of these attacks appears to be replicating or enhancing competing AI systems by reverse-engineering Google's proprietary technology.

Google believes the activity is largely driven by private firms or researchers seeking a competitive edge, though the company declined to identify specific suspects or locations.

John Hultquist, chief analyst at Google's Threat Intelligence Group, warned that such attacks are likely to become more common, particularly as smaller companies deploy customized AI tools trained on valuable or sensitive data.

Major AI developers, including OpenAI, the creator of ChatGPT, have invested billions of dollars in building large language models and consider their internal architectures highly confidential.

OpenAI previously accused Chinese startup DeepSeek of using distillation techniques to improve its own models.

data.title: Vision Pro Users Finally Get Official YouTube App After Two-Year Wait
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Apple's Vision Pro mixed reality headset launched in February 2024, but users were frustrated by the lack of a proper YouTube app. This significant disappointment continued with the release of the second-generation Vision Pro last year.

After two years of silence, an official YouTube app from Google has now launched on the Vision Pro's app store. The app is not just a port of the iPad app; it has panels arranged spatially in front of the user and supports 3D, 360-degree, and 180-degree videos.

According to the app's App Store listing, users can watch 'every video on YouTube,' including a special interface for Shorts vertical videos, and get the 'full signed-in experience' with features like watch history.

Shortly after the Vision Pro's launch, many users complained to YouTube about the lack of an app. They were directed to the web interface, which was an inferior experience, and were told that a Vision Pro app was in the works. However, two years passed before the app finally materialized.

During this time, third-party apps like Juno popped up, but some were later pulled from the App Store on Google's claim that they violated API policies. It's unclear how Google's own XR ambitions may have influenced the development of the Vision Pro app.

While the availability of the official YouTube app is a welcome development for Vision Pro users, the chances of a Netflix app appearing on the platform anytime soon seem slim, as Netflix has not announced any plans to create one.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

data.title: Longeveron Names Willard CEO
data.description:
By Amira McKee

Longeveron named Stephen Willard chief executive officer, effective Feb. 11.

Willard, age 65, succeeds Than Powell as leader of the clinical stage biotechnology company. Powell, who has served as interim CEO since September, will continue his work with the company's on-going business development activities.

Before joining Longeveron, Willard served as chief executive to several biotech companies, including Icapath, NRx Pharmaceuticals, Cellphire, and Flamel Technologies.

In his new role, Willard will receive an annual base salary of $500,000 after an initial period during which he will defer around fifty percent of his base compensation to support the financial needs of the company. Longeveron said it expects that the deferral period will be temporary.

Willard will be eligible to receive short and long-term equity incentive awards including an initial award of 200,000 shares of common stock. His payment package may also include 200,000 restricted stock units and a stock option award exercisable for 200,000 shares, with both vesting quarterly over a four-year period.

Write to Amira McKee at amira.mckee@wsj.com

data.title: Musk's AI chatbot Grok gains US market share amid sexualized images backlash, data shows
data.description:
By Jaspreet Singh and Arsheeya Bajwa

Grok, Elon Musk's AI chatbot, has been gaining ground in the U.S. over the past months, data showed, even as it draws global censure and regulatory scrutiny after being used to generate a wave of non-consensual sexualized images of women and minors.

U.S. market share of the tool rose to 17.8% last month from 14% in December, and 1.9% in January 2025, according to data from research firm Apptopia.

The increase bodes well for money-losing xAI, the Musk-owned startup behind Grok, which was launched about three years ago and has been spending aggressively to scale the infrastructure needed to stay competitive in Silicon Valley's artificial intelligence race.

It comes ahead of an initial public offering of Musk's space firm SpaceX that bought xAI earlier this month to consolidate his empire, and is meant to power his ambitions of putting AI datacenters in orbit.

The deal valued SpaceX at $1 trillion and xAI at $250 billion, Reuters has reported.

The jump firmed Grok - built into Musk's X social media platform - as the third most-used chatbot in the country in January behind market leader OpenAI's ChatGPT and second-placed Google Gemini, according to Apptopia data.

ChatGPT's share slumped to 52.9% last month from 80.9% in January last year, while Gemini's grew to 29.4% from 17.3% over the same period.

"I suspect that cross-promotion with X is the biggest reason for Grok's growth," said Nate Elliott, a principal analyst at Emarketer.

X has integrated Grok across the social media platform, featuring it on the navigation bar and also bundling different tiers of premium Grok access with some of its paid subscriptions.

Grok flooded X last month with AI-altered, near-nude images of real people in response to user requests, triggering global outrage and probes. While curbs X announced stopped Grok's account on the platform from producing such images, the Grok chatbot continues to do so when prompted, Reuters found earlier this month.

X and xAI did not respond to Reuters requests for comment on Friday.

Independent journalist Alex Kantrowitz's Big Technology newsletter first reported the data.

Musk overhauled xAI's management on Wednesday following recent departures of several co-founders that left the startup with only half of its original 12 co-founders and raised questions about stability at the company.

But he cheered the growth in usage at the platform.

"We are also generating 6 billion images in the last 30 days ... Google recently posted that ... 1 billion images were generated using Nano banana in 30 days," an xAI executive said on Wednesday during an all-hands meeting posted on X, comparing xAI's image-generation tool with that of Google's.

"So, you know, we're six times that, right?"

data.title: Lowe's cuts 600 corporate and support roles to focus on store employees
data.description:
Lowe's is eliminating about 600 corporate and support roles, representing less than 1% of the retailer's total workforce, to focus on store employees, a company spokesperson told Reuters on Friday.

"This step helps better align our resources to support our stores and the associates who serve customers every day," the spokesperson said, adding that Lowe's is committed to supporting laid-off employees with financial assistance, continued benefits for a period of time and career transition resources.

The company joins other major U.S. retailers that have recently cut jobs.

Earlier this week, Big-box retailer Target cut around 500 office and supply chain jobs to put money towards more hours for frontline store employees, under new CEO Michael Fiddelke, who had eliminated around 1,800 corporate roles in 2025 — in the company's first major layoff in around a decade.

Lowe's rival Home Depot , in late January, said it was cutting 800 jobs at its Atlanta store support center and called on its corporate employees to return to the office five days a week.

data.title: US Energy Files 8K - Unregistered Equity Sales >USEG
data.description:
U.S. Energy Corp. (USEG) filed a Form 8K - Unregistered Sales of Equity - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on February 13, 2026.

As previously reported in the Current Report on Form 8-K filed by U.S. Energy Corp. (the "Company", "we" and "us") with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "Commission") on October 9, 2025 (the "October 2025 Form 8-K"), on October 9, 2025, the Company entered into a Common Stock Purchase Agreement (the "Purchase Agreement") dated as of October 9, 2025, with Roth Principal Investments, LLC ("Roth Principal Investments"). Upon the terms and subject to the satisfaction of the conditions set forth in the Purchase Agreement, the Company has the right, in its sole discretion, to sell to Roth Principal Investments up to $25,000,000 of shares of the Company's common stock, par value $0.01 per share (the "Common Stock"), subject to certain conditions and limitations contained in the Purchase Agreement, from time to time during the term of the Purchase Agreement.

Since the Company's last periodic report on Form 10-Q filed with the Commission on November 12, 2025, the Company has issued and sold an aggregate of 2,022,539 shares of Common Stock to Roth Principal Investments pursuant to the Purchase Agreement, for aggregate gross proceeds of $2,002,509.10. These issuances represent approximately 5.7% of the Company's outstanding shares of common stock as of September 30, 2025, with a sale transaction completed on February 10, 2026 resulting in the 5% threshold being exceeded and triggering the disclosure requirements of Item 3.02 of Form 8-K.

The shares were issued in a private transaction in reliance upon the exemption from the registration requirements of the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Securities Act"), provided by Section 4(a)(2) of the Securities Act, as a transaction by an issuer not involving a public offering. Roth Principal Investments represented that it is an "accredited investor" as defined in Rule 501(a) of Regulation D under the Securities Act and that it acquired the shares for investment purposes and not with a view to distribution. The Company filed a Form S-1 Registration Statement to register the resale of the shares sold under the Purchase Agreement as discussed above, which was declared effective with the Commission on December 1, 2025.

The foregoing description of the Purchase Agreement does not purport to be complete and is qualified in its entirety by reference to the full text of the Purchase Agreement, which was filed as Exhibit10.1 to the October 2025 Form 8-K, and is incorporated herein by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/101594/000143774926004175/useg20260211_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/101594/000143774926004175/0001437749-26-004175-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.
